q: There are two main types of circulatory system: ___ circulatory system and closed circulatory system.
In an open circulatory system, blood pumped by the ___ heart leaves the open-ended vessels.
When the heart relaxes, the blood flows back into the vessels through pores called ___.
Open circulatory systems are found in most ___ such as insects, spiders and crabs.
In a closed circulatory system, blood is pumped by the heart into the ___.
Main advantages of a closed system are that ___ can be increased and flow can be directed precisely.
Single circulatory system is found in ___.
The fish heart pumps ___ blood to the gills.
In single circulatory systems, blood passes ___ through the heart.
Birds and mammals have evolved the most complex type of transport system known as a ___ circulatory system.
Double circulatory system involves two separate ___.
The ___ circulation carries oxygenated blood from the heart to the body cells.
The ___ circulation carries deoxygenated blood from the heart to the lungs.
Birds and mammals maintain a ___ body temperature.
Double circulation ensure that the oxygenated and deoxygenated blood cannot ___.
